transactions.desc: The Mets optioned Wagaman to Triple-A Syracuse on Monday.
The Mets reinstated Robert (back) from the 60-day injured list Monday.
The Dodgers optioned Hurt to Triple-A Oklahoma City on Monday.
The Phillies optioned Rangel to Triple-A Lehigh Valley on Monday.
The Phillies recalled McFarlane from Double-A Reading on Monday.
The Twins optioned Gray to Triple-A St. Paul on Monday.
The Twins activated Buxton (hip) from the 10-day injured list Monday.
The Rays optioned Englert to Triple-A Durham on Saturday.
The Yankees optioned Rodriguez to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Monday.
Celesten was promoted to Double-A Arkansas on Sunday, Adam Jude of The Seattle Times reports.
The Angels reinstated Campero (hand) from the 10-day injured list and optioned him to Triple-A Salt Lake on Sunday.
The Mets outrighted Short to Triple-A Syracuse on Sunday.
King was optioned to Triple-A Sugar Land after Sunday's 5-2 loss to the Orioles, Matt Kawahara of the Houston Chronicle reports.
Coleman was promoted to Triple-A Buffalo on Sunday, Mitch Bannon of The Athletic reports.
The Nationals re-signed Krook to a major-league contract prior to Sunday's game against the Athletics.
The Yankees recalled Rodriguez from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre ahead of the second game of Sunday's doubleheader versus the Dodgers.
The Yankees optioned Bird to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Sunday.
The Yankees recalled Castro from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Sunday.
The Dodgers recalled Hurt from Triple-A Oklahoma City to serve as their 27th man for the second game of Sunday's doubleheader with the Yankees, Maddie Lee of the Los Angeles Times reports.
Detroit selected Watson's contract from Triple-A Toledo and optioned him back to Toledo on Sunday.
Baltimore optioned Sanders to Triple-A Norfolk on Sunday.
Baltimore selected Huff's contract from Triple-A Norfolk on Sunday.
The Padres optioned Brito to Triple-A El Paso on Sunday.
San Diego reinstated Peralta (personal) from the bereavement list Sunday.
San Diego reinstated Vasquez (ankle) from the 15-day injured list Sunday.
Houston designated Pearson for assignment on Sunday, Matt Kawahara of the Houston Chronicle reports.
The Astros recalled Delgado from Triple-A Sugar Land on Sunday, Chandler Rome of The Athletic reports.

Manager Aaron Boone said Rodriguez, who was recalled from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre to serve as the 27th man for Sunday's doubleheader against the Dodgers, will "potentially" start Game 2 of the twin bill, Gary Phillips of the New York Daily News reports.
Miami optioned Hinds to Jacksonville on Sunday.
The Marlins recalled Pauley from Triple-A Jacksonville on Sunday.
